aggregation_rules.note: Future aggregators must never treat intended claim text as demonstrated evidence

evidence_strength_taxonomy_invariant.statement: Evidence-strength levels must be assigned from the semantic claim actually demonstrated, not from the presence of a particular test operation.
operations_do_not_automatically_upgrade_: running on another process or platform
evidence_strength_taxonomy_invariant.not_a_universal_linear_ladder: InterfaceBehavior, LogicalStateTransition, ProcessCrashRecovery, FilesystemDurability, HardwarePowerLoss, and CrossPlatform are not a universal linear maturity ladder for every scenario. A scenario may be fully correct at InterfaceBehavior without needing to target a higher level.

no_transition_invariant_rule.statement: A scenario that verifies no mutation occurred is not automatically a LogicalStateTransition.
no_transition_invariant_rule.evidence_strength_assignment: Assign strength based on the specific semantic claim demonstrated, not merely because before equals after.

contracts.intended_claim: Create, open, read normalized canonical state, and close without mutation; optionally verify persisted initialization reload through close and reopen.
contracts.current_demonstrated_claim: Create, open, read normalized canonical state, and close in one process without observing a close-to-reopen reload.
contracts.pre_state: Empty storage root; fixture normalized state as create input
contracts.persisted_observation: Session artifacts exist on disk after create; state readable while handle open
contracts.expected_result: Read state matches fixture normalized oracle
forbidden_shortcuts: classify close/reopen as authoritative logical state transition
contracts.current_assertion_summary: passed_oracle(fixture.normalized_state(), actual) after create/open/read/close in same process
related_higher_level_claims_requiring_se: Persisted initialization reload through close and reopen, remaining InterfaceBehavior under the current evidence taxonomy
ProcessCrashRecovery via explicit crash/interruption scenario
LogicalStateTransition requires a separate explicit authoritative mutation scenario
contracts.current_gap: No close-to-reopen cycle; proves API flow not persisted reload

contracts.intended_claim: Persist one representative ReviewLedger correction event across close and reopen.
contracts.current_demonstrated_claim: Apply one ReviewLedger correction command and observe the resulting normalized state through the same open handle.
contracts.pre_state: Created session with fixture canonical state loaded
contracts.persisted_observation: State after command via same-handle read_normalized_state
contracts.reopen_or_recovery_step: Not performed; handle closed without reopen
contracts.expected_result: Post-command state matches expected mutation built via shared apply_command
forbidden_shortcuts: expected built with same apply path as adapter
contracts.current_assertion_summary: passed_oracle(expected.normalize(), actual) after apply and same-handle read
related_higher_level_claims_requiring_se: ProcessCrashRecovery via explicit crash/interruption during or after commit
FilesystemDurability via physical/filesystem fault scenario
contracts.current_gap: No close-to-reopen; expected/actual share semantic_ops blind spot

contracts.intended_claim: Persist one immutable analysis result across close and reopen.
contracts.current_demonstrated_claim: Apply one analysis attachment command and observe the resulting normalized state through the same open handle.
contracts.pre_state: Created session with fixture canonical state including existing analysis results
contracts.persisted_observation: State after command via same-handle read
contracts.expected_result: Analysis result present with preserved identities and ordering
contracts.current_assertion_summary: passed_oracle(expected_state, actual) after apply; sorted analysis_results
related_higher_level_claims_requiring_se: ProcessCrashRecovery via explicit crash/interruption during or after commit
FilesystemDurability via physical/filesystem fault scenario
contracts.current_gap: No close-to-reopen; shared apply_command for expected

contracts.intended_claim: Reject a stale ReviewLedger command without mutating canonical state, with independent persisted before/after observation.
contracts.current_demonstrated_claim: Reject a stale ReviewLedger command with stale-precondition and observe unchanged normalized state through the same open handle before and after the rejected command.
contracts.pre_state: Writable session with loaded canonical state
contracts.persisted_observation: before and after state via same handle
contracts.expected_result: Error code stale-precondition; before equals after
forbidden_shortcuts: treat no-mutation check as automatic LogicalStateTransition

no_transition_invariant.independent_persisted_observation: same-handle before/after read only; not independent persisted reopen
no_transition_invariant.no_transition_invariant_tested: canonical state unchanged after rejected command

related_higher_level_claims_requiring_se: Independent persisted before/after observation via close and reopen
ConcurrentAccess multi-actor workload scenario
contracts.current_gap: FailureModel::ConcurrentAccess overstates single-handle scoped precondition test

contracts.intended_claim: Permit at most one authoritative writer; second attempt rejected safely with independent persisted observation that canonical state is unchanged.
contracts.current_demonstrated_claim: Reject a second writable open with writer-already-open while the first handle is held; canonical state is not independently verified unchanged.
contracts.pre_state: Created session; first writable handle open
contracts.operation: attempt second open(Writable) while first handle held
contracts.persisted_observation: before state read; second open result
contracts.reopen_or_recovery_step: Not performed; no crash recovery test
contracts.expected_result: Second open returns writer-already-open; canonical state unchanged
forbidden_shortcuts: treat rejection alone as no-transition LogicalStateTransition proof

no_transition_invariant.independent_persisted_observation: self-compare before/before only; not independent persisted observation
no_transition_invariant.no_transition_invariant_tested: intended but not validly demonstrated due to self-oracle

related_higher_level_claims_requiring_se: Independent persisted before/after observation that canonical state remains unchanged
ProcessCrashRecovery for abandoned writer and orphan-lock recovery
contracts.current_gap: Self-oracle; no abandoned writer or crash-held lock recovery

contracts.intended_claim: Reject writable open for unknown newer session format with exact error classification; optionally support read-only salvage as a separate claim.
contracts.current_demonstrated_claim: Writable open fails with unsupported-newer-format after pre-open format mutation; no read-only salvage path tested.
contracts.pre_state: Created session; format_version bumped to newer unsupported value

no_transition_invariant.independent_persisted_observation: error code only; no persisted state comparison
no_transition_invariant.no_transition_invariant_tested: writable open rejected; no mutation attempted

related_higher_level_claims_requiring_se: Read-only salvage path as separate claim and scenario
CrossPlatform via execution on multiple platforms
contracts.current_gap: Tautological oracle; no read-only salvage path tested

intended_claims.claim: Detect or classify derived-state corruption while preserving access to canonical state.
Rebuild derived state and verify the rebuilt artifact after close and reopen.

contracts.current_demonstrated_claim: Canonical state remains readable after modifying an artifact designated as derived, but the handler does not observe, classify, or rebuild that derived artifact.
contracts.pre_state: Created session with canonical and derived artifacts on disk
contracts.operation: corrupt derived artifact then open(ReadOnly) and read normalized state
contracts.persisted_observation: Canonical state readable; derived corruption not consulted
contracts.reopen_or_recovery_step: Open read-only only; no derived rebuild asserted
contracts.expected_result: Canonical oracle matches fixture; derived detection/rebuild as separate subclaims
contracts.required_error_classification: none for read-only open success path
forbidden_shortcuts: treat canonical readability alone as rebuildability evidence
related_higher_level_claims_requiring_se: derived_corruption_detection subclaim
derived_state_rebuild subclaim
ProcessCrashRecovery if corruption occurs during write
contracts.current_gap: Current handler supports neither full intended subclaim; canonical readability alone is not rebuildability evidence

contracts.intended_claim: Detect broken canonical reference and fail closed on writable open with exact error classification; verify canonical state unchanged via independent persisted observation.
contracts.current_demonstrated_claim: Writable open fails on corrupted canonical reference; any error accepted; no independent persisted before/after observation.
contracts.pre_state: Created session; canonical reference corrupted in persisted state
contracts.expected_result: Writable open fails with specific canonical-corruption classification
forbidden_shortcuts: upgrade exact error classification to LogicalStateTransition mechanically

no_transition_invariant.exact_error_or_policy_result: any Err accepted in current handler; intended specific canonical-corruption code
no_transition_invariant.no_transition_invariant_tested: intended writable-open rejection without mutation; not validly demonstrated

contracts.current_assertion_summary: Any Err on writable open then passed_oracle(fixture, fixture)
related_higher_level_claims_requiring_se: Exact canonical-corruption error classification: InterfaceBehavior
Canonical state remains unchanged after rejected writable open: requires independent persisted before/after observation; do not assign a higher evidence level mechanically
Read-only salvage path as separate claim
contracts.current_gap: Weak error classification; tautological oracle; no independent persisted observation

contracts.intended_claim: Create new session identity with source lineage; copied canonical history oracle-valid against independent expected values after persisted reopen of both sessions.
contracts.current_demonstrated_claim: Duplicate session returns new session id and lineage metadata; oracle self-compares duplicate.normalized_state; neither source nor duplicate reopened from persisted storage.
contracts.pre_state: Source session created and opened writable
contracts.reopen_or_recovery_step: Neither source nor duplicate reopened from persisted storage
contracts.expected_result: Independent session ids; lineage metadata; both sessions oracle-valid against independent expected
contracts.current_assertion_summary: Checks duplicate_id and duplicated_from; passed_oracle(duplicate, duplicate)
related_higher_level_claims_requiring_se: Source and duplicate persisted reopen with independent expected values
Source unchanged verification and divergent mutation test
ProcessCrashRecovery during duplication
contracts.current_gap: Self-oracle; no persisted reopen; source mutation not tested

contracts.intended_claim: Interrupt authoritative transition around durable acknowledgement; no partial state exposed; verify last committed state after recovery.
contracts.current_demonstrated_claim: Logical pre-commit abort before any write; command fails with simulated-durability-failure; post-reopen read-only state equals pre-command state.
contracts.fault_point: before any write or transaction begin
contracts.persisted_observation: Reopen read-only and compare to before state
contracts.reopen_or_recovery_step: close -> reopen(Writable) -> apply under fault -> close -> reopen(ReadOnly)
contracts.expected_result: Command fails; post-reopen state equals last committed state
forbidden_shortcuts: treat unchanged state after pre-write abort as ProcessCrashRecovery

no_transition_invariant.independent_persisted_observation: reopen read-only after abort; before equals after
no_transition_invariant.no_transition_invariant_tested: no authoritative transition committed; state unchanged after logical pre-commit abort

contracts.current_assertion_summary: Err(simulated-durability-failure) then passed_oracle(before, after) after reopen
related_higher_level_claims_requiring_se: ProcessCrashRecovery via mid-write and post-ack fault points
FilesystemDurability via physical/filesystem fault scenario
contracts.current_gap: Fault fires before mutation; proves logical abort not crash recovery

contracts.intended_claim: Interrupted compaction does not expose mixed or invalid canonical state; verify valid committed state after recovery.
contracts.current_demonstrated_claim: Pre-compaction interrupt returns simulated-compaction-interrupt before any compaction mutation; no post-fault persisted-state observation.
contracts.pre_state: Created session; compaction capability supported; fault armed FailCompaction
contracts.expected_result: Compaction aborts safely; reopen shows valid committed state matching pre-compaction oracle

no_transition_invariant.no_transition_invariant_tested: compaction aborted before mutation; no state change expected or observed

contracts.current_assertion_summary: Err(simulated-compaction-interrupt) then passed_oracle(fixture, fixture); no compaction mutation observed
related_higher_level_claims_requiring_se: Post-interrupt reopen and state validation after in-progress compaction mutation
ProcessCrashRecovery during in-progress compaction mutation
contracts.current_gap: Invalid or insufficient for compaction recovery; no compaction write occurred; no post-fault state validation

contracts.intended_claim: Interrupt destructive historical cleanup without silent canonical corruption; verify canonical state valid after recovery.
contracts.current_demonstrated_claim: Scenario not executed; destructive historical GC capability not implemented in spike adapters.
contracts.pre_state: Session with destructive GC capability required
contracts.expected_result: Cleanup interrupt recoverable; canonical state valid after reopen
related_higher_level_claims_requiring_se: Deferred: cleanup interruption recovery after DestructiveHistoricalGc capability exists and is separately authorized
deferred_requirements_after_capability: Implement destructive historical GC capability in candidate adapters
Add interrupt fault during cleanup execution
Reopen and validate canonical state after interrupt
contracts.current_gap: Capability not implemented; correctly Unsupported not executed
